task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset is loaded in Oracle Analytics Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Oracle Analytics Desktop is open and 'Sample Order Lines' is visible in the Data Elements panel on the left.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the first map layer using 'State' and 'Sales'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map visualization is present on the canvas. In the Grammar/Data panel for the first layer, 'State' is assigned to the Location/Category field and 'Sales' is assigned to the Color field. The map displays filled shapes for states.

task_completion.description: Verify the addition of the second map layer using 'City' and 'Profit'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Grammar/Data panel shows a second layer (or multiple layers) configured. For this layer, 'City' is assigned to the Location field and 'Profit' is assigned to the Size or Color field. The map displays points (cities) overlaid on top of the state shapes.

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is being saved to the correct path with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog is visible, showing the target directory 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' and the filename 'Geospatial_Analysis'.

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook has been successfully saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Oracle Analytics Desktop window title or top header displays 'Geospatial_Analysis', or a File Explorer window shows the 'Geospatial_Analysis.dva' file present in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to build the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent dragging and dropping fields, clicking through menus, and interacting with the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface, rather than using a terminal or script to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the map visualization is genuinely rendered by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map should appear integrated within the Oracle Analytics Desktop canvas with native UI elements (like zoom controls or tooltips), not as an imported image or external window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually write the .dva file contents in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editors (like Notepad or VS Code) open with XML/JSON code attempting to mock the .dva file structure.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a map visualization in Oracle Analytics Desktop using the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset. Layer 1 must use 'State' for location and 'Sales' for color (filled shapes). Layer 2 must use 'City' for location and 'Profit' for size/color (points). The final workbook must be saved exactly as 'Geospatial_Analysis.dva' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents'.
